quests.title: Welcome to the Machine
quests.description: The Iron Hegemony does not recruit — it conscripts, shapes, and deploys. Warlord Harkon Steele built the Hegemony from the Concordium's security division after the Great Schism, because he understood something the diplomats didn't: Prima Materia cannot be negotiated with. It can only be controlled by force. The Scrap Field is your proving ground — Broken Scout Drones are jamming logistics, and in the Hegemony, a soldier who can't clear the field is not a soldier at all. Quest giver: Commander Drax at Bastion Core Command Bunker.

objectives.desc: Collect salvage parts for the war machine.

quests.title: No Weakness in the Supply Line
quests.description: Scrap Rats have been gnawing through the supply crates the Hegemony uses to move components between the Scrap Field and Bastion Core. In any other organization, this would be a maintenance problem. In the Iron Hegemony, it is a tactical failure — every gap in the supply line is a gap in the war machine's armor. Warlord Steele has a saying: 'The rat that eats your rations today is the enemy that kills your unit tomorrow.' Exterminate them. Quest giver: Commander Drax at Bastion Core Command Bunker.

objectives.desc: Collect Rat tails for bounty proof.

quests.title: The Forge Must Be Fed
quests.description: Tink — the Hegemony's salvage engineer, a person who hasn't slept a full night in six years because the Bastion forges never sleep either — needs Scrap Drone components to keep the war machine running. (Location: Scrap Field, Tink) The Hegemony's strength is not just in soldiers; it is in the relentless industrial capacity that turns scrap into weapons. Every drone you disable is another batch of components that becomes a new piece of armor, a new weapon, another tool of control.

quests.title: Iron Does Not Rust
quests.description: Rust Mites have colonized the Hegemony's equipment depot at the Scrap Field perimeter — eating through iron plating that Bastion engineers spent weeks fabricating. In the Hegemony's ideology, corrosion is not just a material problem; it is a symbol of the weakness that allowed the Great Schism to happen. Warlord Steele demands that the Scrap Field be maintained in fighting condition at all times. A soldier who lets their equipment rot is a soldier who does not believe in the mission. Quest giver: Commander Drax at Bastion Core Command Bunker.

quests.title: Forged in Bastion Steel
quests.description: Warlord Harkon Steele reviews every conscript personally — or so the rumor goes. What is true is that a summons to Bastion Core means someone has noticed you are more than standard material. The Hegemony does not believe in generalist soldiers; every weapon has a function, and a soldier who has not found theirs is a weapon without an edge. At Bastion Core's war hall, you will choose the form your lethality takes. Choose it correctly. There are no second specializations. Quest giver: War Marshal Drax at Bastion Core War College.

objectives.desc: Report to War Marshal Drax at the Bastion.
objectives.description: Speak with npc hegemony class trainer. (War Marshal Drax at Bastion Core War College)

objectives.desc: Demonstrate your worth against Scrap Hounds.

quests.title: The Ravine Is Ours Now
quests.description: The Iron Ravine sits on mineral deposits that could supply the Bastion's forges for a generation — deposits the Concordium surveyed and filed away while debating policy, and that the Meridian Cartel is already sending surveyors to assess. Warlord Steele does not debate. He sends soldiers. The Iron Hegemony's doctrine is simple: the one who controls the mineral controls the war machine, and the one who controls the war machine controls everything else. Establish the forward base. Quest giver: Commander Drax at Bastion Core Command Bunker.

objectives.description: Travel to IRON RAVINE. (Travel to: Iron Ravine)

quests.title: Competing Claims Are Not Claims
quests.description: Iron Ravine Miners — independent operators who have worked these veins for years, who have no Hegemony contracts and no Hegemony protection — are blocking access to the central mineral shaft. In the Concordium, this would be a dispute. In the Meridian Cartel, this would be a negotiation. In the Iron Hegemony, Warlord Steele has a single word for independent operators who stand between his soldiers and a strategic resource: obstacles. Quest giver: Gorn at Iron Ravine Mine Entrance.

objectives.desc: Collect Ravine Ore for the forges.

quests.title: A Lesson in Superior Force
quests.description: An Iron Ravine Brute has claimed the central shaft as its territory and crushed three of the Hegemony's initial mining crews — not out of intelligence but pure animal dominance. Warlord Steele has a complicated respect for dominant animals; they understand, on instinct, what the Concordium's diplomats will never accept. But respect does not mean tolerance. The Ravine belongs to the Hegemony. Show the Brute what superior force looks like from the losing side. Quest giver: Commander Drax at Bastion Core Command Bunker.

objectives.desc: Claim the Brute's weapon as trophy.

quests.title: There Is Only One Authority Here
quests.description: A Scrapfield Enforcer — a rogue combat machine that predates the Hegemony's own war-mech program — has been claiming the central ravine as its patrol zone and attacking Hegemony survey teams on sight. Its combat protocols may actually be superior to some Hegemony-built machines, which Tink finds professionally embarrassing and Warlord Steele finds unacceptable. The Iron Hegemony is the only authority in the Iron Ravine. Enforce that fact. Quest giver: Commander Drax at Bastion Core Command Bunker.

objectives.desc: Destroy a Ravine Sentinel guarding the Enforcer.

quests.title: Strip Them to the Core
quests.description: Ravine Scrap Golems are what happens when the Severance's corrupted energy animates salvaged machinery without anyone controlling the result — walking fortresses of mismatched metal and raw aethel charge that will attack anything within range. (Location: Iron Ravine, Commander Drax) Warlord Steele does not waste time theorizing about what they are. He looks at them and sees two things: a threat to neutralize, and a salvage yield that Tink has already calculated down to the last circuit. Break them. Take what the Hegemony needs.

quests.title: What Wars Leave Behind
quests.description: The War Machine Graveyard is a cemetery of titans — pre-Severance war constructs that fought wars whose names have been forgotten, left to rust in a field that no other faction wants to enter. Warlord Steele walks it differently. He sees it as a library, every destroyed machine a lesson about what failed and why. The Iron Hegemony's doctrine forbids forgetting the last war. Enter the Graveyard. See what losing looks like, so you understand what winning requires. Quest giver: Commander Drax at Bastion Core Command Bunker.

objectives.description: Travel to WAR MACHINE GRAVEYARD. (Travel to: War Machine Graveyard)

quests.title: The Graveyard Has One Owner
quests.description: War Machine Wreckers — scavengers who strip the Graveyard's titan remains for parts to sell to the Meridian Cartel and OmniCorp — have been operating here for years without Hegemony authorization. (Location: War Machine Graveyard, Foreman Brigg) Foreman Brigg's inventory assessments show they've already taken components that the Hegemony's Titan Assembly Yard needed. The Graveyard's lessons belong to the Iron Hegemony. Its parts belong to the Iron Hegemony. Its scavengers belong on the ground.

objectives.desc: Collect usable parts from destroyed Wreckers.

quests.title: Tink's Inventory List
quests.description: Tink — whose actual feelings about the War Machine Graveyard are somewhere between reverence and obsession — has produced a precise inventory of components the Hegemony needs and the War Machine Scavengers are taking. The Scavengers are not random looters; they are organized, they have buyers, and their operation is directly funded by OmniCorp. Every scrap core they remove is a weapon the Hegemony does not build. Tink says 'please.' Warlord Steele says 'eliminate them.'

quests.title: Reverse-Engineer the Past
quests.description: War Machine Sentinels — automated guardian constructs still running on battle-programming written before the Severance — continue patrolling the Graveyard as if the war they were built for is still ongoing. (Location: War Machine Graveyard, Foreman Brigg) Tink has analyzed their combat protocols and found techniques the Hegemony's current Titan Assembly Yard has not re-discovered. Disable them carefully. Every Sentinel destroyed is a lesson extracted from the past to make the Hegemony's future war machines more lethal.

quests.title: Full Tactical Suppression
quests.description: Warlord Steele does not believe in partial victories. A contested zone is a weak zone — every Wrecker and Scavenger still active in the Graveyard represents a hole in the Hegemony's tactical control. The full sweep order is simple and absolute: everything in the Graveyard that is not Iron Hegemony is either neutralized or tagged for disposal. By the end of this operation, the War Machine Graveyard will be an Iron Hegemony asset with no asterisks. Quest giver: Commander Drax at Bastion Core Command Bunker.

quests.title: Target Practice
quests.description: Multiple War Machine Sentinels are converging on the Hegemony's salvage teams — an automated counter-response to the Hegemony's presence in their patrol zones. (Location: War Machine Graveyard, Commander Drax) Warlord Steele's briefing is characteristically brief: 'Multiple Sentinels converging. This is not a threat. This is target practice.' The Hegemony's soldiers are not told to be careful. They are told to be efficient. Efficiency is the highest virtue in the Iron Hegemony's doctrine of war.

quests.title: The Bastion Needs Feeding
quests.description: Foreman Brigg has a requisition list from the Bastion's Titan Assembly program — specific components from specific models in the Graveyard that cannot be manufactured any other way. (Location: War Machine Graveyard, Foreman Brigg) The program is behind schedule. Three rival factions are ahead of the Hegemony in their own advanced weapons programs, and Warlord Steele does not tolerate being behind. The Graveyard Wreckers are in the retrieval zones again. Remove them. Retrieve what the Bastion needs. Bring the Hegemony back ahead.

quests.title: The Graveyard Has a Commander
quests.description: Deep in the War Machine Graveyard's protected core zone, a Sentinel Commander — a war construct with hierarchical battle protocols that can coordinate the scattered Sentinels into a unified tactical force — has activated. Tink believes it has been dormant for centuries, triggered by the Hegemony's salvage operations. Warlord Steele believes this is irrelevant. The Graveyard belongs to the Iron Hegemony. Destroy the Commander. Plant the claim beacon. End this. Quest giver: Commander Drax at Bastion Core Command Bunker.

objectives.desc: Destroy the Sentinel Commander and its unit.

quests.title: No Anomalies in Our Territory
quests.description: A Resonance Echo — the kind of energy entity the Arcanum Seekers would write excited papers about — has formed in the Graveyard's deep sector, drawn by the resonance from the now-active Sentinel Commander. (Location: War Machine Graveyard, Commander Drax) It is disrupting every piece of Hegemony electronic equipment within range and coordinating erratic behavior in the surviving Sentinels. The Iron Hegemony does not tolerate anomalies in controlled territory. This is controlled territory now. Destroy it.

quests.title: The Bastion Recognizes You
quests.description: The War Machine Graveyard is secured. The Titan Assembly program has its components. Warlord Harkon Steele does not give compliments — he gives promotions, which mean exactly the same thing in the Iron Hegemony. Return to Bastion Core for your Advanced Combat elevation. The Titan Assembly Yard awaits, and what is built there will change the balance of power on Xylos. You are going to help build it. Quest giver: Commander Drax at Bastion Core Command Bunker.

objectives.description: Travel to BASTION CORE. (Travel to: Bastion Core)

quests.title: Iron Incursion
quests.description: The Titan Assembly Yard was once where Precursor war machines were born. The Hegemony will make it our primary production facility. Quest giver: Foreman Brigg at Titan Assembly Yard Command Post.

objectives.description: Travel to TITAN ASSEMBLY YARD. (Travel to: Titan Assembly Yard)

quests.title: Drone Sweep
quests.description: Titan Assembly Drones still function on ancient protocols. (Location: Titan Assembly Yard, Foreman Brigg) Override their programming with force.

quests.title: Sentinel Outpost
quests.description: Titan Assembly Sentinels guard the central assembly line. (Location: Titan Assembly Yard, Commander Drax) Breach their defenses and claim the line for Hegemony production.

objectives.desc: Seize the assembly line control terminal.

quests.title: Forge Automaton
quests.description: Titan Forge Automatons are apex construction machines. (Location: Titan Assembly Yard, Foreman Brigg) Disable them and study their assembly protocols.

quests.title: Proto Core
quests.description: A Titan Proto Core — a failed Titan — has reactivated. (Location: Titan Assembly Yard, Commander Drax) This is a live-fire exercise. Destroy it.

quests.title: Skirmish Ring
quests.description: The Border Skirmish Ring is where factions test their strength. The Hegemony does not test — we dominate. Quest giver: Commander Drax at Bastion Core Command Bunker.

objectives.description: Travel to BORDER SKIRMISH RING. (Travel to: Border Skirmish Ring)

quests.title: Border Patrol
quests.description: Patrol the skirmish ring and eliminate any non-Hegemony forces. (Location: Border Skirmish Ring, Commander Drax) The border is ours.

objectives.desc: Complete border patrol and claim the badge.

quests.title: Behemoth Assault
quests.description: Aethel Behemoths are being used as living siege weapons by rival factions. (Location: Border Skirmish Ring, Commander Drax) Destroy the weapon before it reaches Hegemony lines.

objectives.desc: Destroy the enemy Behemoth siege units.

objectives.description: Collect raw aethel core (x1). [Item: Raw Aethel Core]

quests.title: Sentinel Assembly
quests.description: The Assembly Yard has produced new Sentinel variants. (Location: Titan Assembly Yard, Foreman Brigg) Test Hegemony combat doctrine against them.

quests.title: Ascension Test
quests.description: War Marshal Drax has called for your Ascension Test. (Location: Bastion Core, War Marshal Drax) Prove your strength has surpassed the rank of common soldier.

objectives.description: Speak with npc hegemony class trainer. (War Marshal Drax at Bastion Core War College)

objectives.desc: Defeat the Resonance Echo as your strength test.
Destroy a Titan Proto Core as your endurance test.

quests.title: Skies of Iron
quests.description: The Hegemony now controls the ground. The next frontier is the sky. Secure the Orbital Sentinel Command. Quest giver: Commander Drax at Bastion Core Command Bunker.

objectives.description: Travel to ORBITAL SENTINEL COMMAND. (Travel to: Orbital Sentinel Command)

objectives.desc: Deploy the Hegemony orbital control beacon.

quests.title: Drake Recon
quests.description: The Ashen Drake threatens Hegemony air superiority. (Location: Cradle Expanse, Commander Drax) Conduct reconnaissance and engage if necessary.

objectives.desc: Engage the Ashen Drake for reconnaissance.

quests.title: Iron Carapace Breach
quests.description: Aethel Iron Carapaces are armored threats. (Location: Cradle Expanse, Commander Drax) Hegemony tanks need live-fire practice. Be the gun.

objectives.desc: Harvest armor plates for the forges.

quests.title: Behemoth Target
quests.description: Aethel Corrupted Behemoths are priority targets. (Location: Cradle Expanse, Commander Drax) Any threat to Hegemony territory receives the same response: elimination.

quests.title: Abyssal Front
quests.description: Aethel Abyssal Reavers are the enemy's shock troops. (Location: Cradle Expanse, Commander Drax) Meet them with Hegemony steel.

objectives.description: Collect void channeler core (x1). [Item: Void Channeler Core]

quests.title: Orbital Defense Grid
quests.description: Rival factions probe the orbital defense grid. (Location: Orbital Sentinel Command, Commander Drax) Remind them why Hegemony controls the skies.

objectives.desc: Neutralize void threats to the grid.

quests.title: Drake Hunt
quests.description: The Ashen Drake has been sighted again. (Location: Cradle Expanse, Commander Drax) This time, Hegemony authorizes a kill order. Bring it down.

objectives.desc: Hunt and kill the Ashen Drake.

quests.title: Ground Assault
quests.description: Full ground assault on enemy positions in the Skirmish Ring. (Location: Border Skirmish Ring, Commander Drax) Hegemony doctrine: overwhelming force.

quests.title: Abyssal Counter-Offensive
quests.description: The Abyss Counter-Offensive has begun. (Location: Cradle Expanse, Commander Drax) Multiple Reaver battalions advance on Hegemony positions. Break their offensive.

quests.title: Master Commander
quests.description: The Master Commander trial. (Location: Bastion Core, Commander Drax) Defeat the Ashen Drake and two elite guardians. Only the strongest lead Hegemony forces into battle.

quests.title: Subjugating the Chasm
quests.description: The Void Echo Chasm is the most hostile environment on Xylos. The Hegemony will establish a forward base here. Fear is not an option. Quest giver: Commander Drax at Bastion Core Command Bunker.

objectives.description: Travel to VOID ECHO CHASM. (Travel to: Void Echo Chasm)
Collect void channeler core (x2). [Item: Void Channeler Core]

quests.title: Reaver Engagement
quests.description: Void Reavers are the chasm's defenders. (Location: Void Echo Chasm, Commander Drax) Engage and destroy. Hegemony fears no darkness.

quests.title: Channeler Purge
quests.description: Void Channelers perform rituals that empower the Abyss. (Location: Void Echo Chasm, Commander Drax) Purge them. The Hegemony leaves no enemy power source intact.

objectives.desc: Purge Void Channelers and their rituals.

objectives.description: Collect raw aethel fragment (x1). [Item: Raw Aethel Fragment]

quests.title: Abyssal Extermination
quests.description: Abyssal Reavers are the enemy's elite. (Location: Void Echo Chasm, Commander Drax) Exterminate them. The Hegemony tolerates no elite but its own.

objectives.description: Collect void channeler core (x1). [Item: Void Channeler Core]

quests.title: Behemoth Elimination
quests.description: Corrupted Behemoths in the chasm are juiced on void energy. (Location: Void Echo Chasm, Commander Drax) Prove that Hegemony steel cuts deeper than void corruption.

objectives.desc: Collect void-resistance data for armor research.

quests.title: Soul Breaker
quests.description: Soul Fragmenters attempt to break the minds of Hegemony soldiers. (Location: Void Echo Chasm, Commander Drax) They will fail. Destroy these psychological warfare units.

quests.title: Chrono Elimination
quests.description: Chrono Wraiths warp time around Hegemony positions. (Location: Void Echo Chasm, Commander Drax) Counter their temporal attacks with overwhelming firepower.

quests.title: Jotunn Break
quests.description: A Gigantic Jotunn blocks the advance. (Location: Void Echo Chasm, Commander Drax) Hegemony doctrine: destroy the obstacle and keep moving.

objectives.desc: Deploy advance beacon past the Jotunn.

quests.title: Heart Breach
quests.description: The Shattered Heart is the enemy's core territory. Breach it. The Hegemony attacks — always. Quest giver: Commander Drax at Bastion Core Command Bunker.

objectives.description: Travel to SHATTERED HEART. (Travel to: Shattered Heart, Shattered Heart)

quests.title: The Drake's Fall
quests.description: THE DRAKE EXECUTION. The Ashen Drake rules the Shattered Heart. The Hegemony will execute it. Raid Tier-1: lead the assault and bring down the Drake. Victory through Conquest. Quest giver: Commander Drax at Bastion Core Command Bunker.

objectives.desc: Clear Drake Minions from the lair.
Claim the Drake's head as trophy for the Bastion.

quests.title: Void Warfare
quests.description: The Soul Fragmenter threat has escalated. (Location: Void Echo Chasm, Commander Drax) Multiple units are deploying. This is now a military campaign.

objectives.desc: Submit campaign status to High Command.

quests.title: Total Void Control
quests.description: Operation Total Void Control is authorized. (Location: Void Echo Chasm, Commander Drax) Exterminate all void signatures in the chasm. The Hegemony does not share territory.

quests.title: Void Stalker Execution
quests.description: A Void Stalker has been hunting our soldiers. (Location: Void Echo Chasm, Commander Drax) This insult to Hegemony strength will be answered with execution.

quests.title: Temporal Breach
quests.description: Chrono Wraiths create temporal breaches that trap our troops. (Location: Void Echo Chasm, Commander Drax) Close the breaches with extreme prejudice.

objectives.desc: Destroy Chrono Wraiths and close breaches.

quests.title: Heart Assault
quests.description: Full assault on the Shattered Heart. Enemy Reaver battalions are dug in. Dig them out. Quest giver: Commander Drax at Bastion Core Command Bunker.

quests.title: Jotunn Strongpoint
quests.description: Gigantic Jotunns have fortified a position in the Heart. (Location: Shattered Heart, Commander Drax) Hegemony doctrine for strongpoints: overwhelming force.

quests.title: Tyrant Battery
quests.description: Crystal Tyrants form a defensive battery in the Heart. (Location: Shattered Heart, Commander Drax) Silence their guns.

quests.title: Harbinger Beachhead
quests.description: The Abyss Harbinger is the enemy high command. (Location: Void Echo Chasm, Commander Drax) Establish a beachhead for the final assault.

objectives.desc: Eliminate Chrono Wraith defending the beachhead.

objectives.description: Collect raw aethel fragment (x1). [Item: Raw Aethel Fragment]

quests.title: Fragmenter Front
quests.description: Soul Fragmenters form the enemy's psychological warfare front. (Location: Void Echo Chasm, Commander Drax) Break their front.

quests.title: Harbinger's Defeat
quests.description: FINAL ASSAULT ON THE ABYSS. (Location: Void Echo Chasm, Commander Drax) The Abyss Harbinger is the enemy commander. Destroy it and claim total victory over the Void. THE HEGEMONY CONTROLS ALL.

objectives.desc: Claim the Harbinger's battle standard for the Bastion.

quests.title: Stabilizing the Front
quests.description: The Severance Faultline threatens planetary stability. The Hegemony does not negotiate with nature — we conquer it. Quest giver: Commander Drax at Bastion Core Command Bunker.

objectives.description: Travel to SEVERANCE FAULTLINE. (Travel to: Severance Faultline, Shattered Heart)

quests.title: Carapace Armor
quests.description: Aethel Iron Carapaces have armor that rivals Hegemony steel. (Location: Severance Faultline, Commander Drax) Destroy them and study their composition.

objectives.desc: Collect armor samples for the forges.

quests.title: Tyrant Position
quests.description: Crystal Tyrants have taken positions along the fault. (Location: Severance Faultline, Commander Drax) They are obstacles. Remove them.

quests.title: Jotunn Blockade
quests.description: Gigantic Jotunns blockade the main advance route. (Location: Severance Faultline, Commander Drax) Break through. Nothing blocks the Hegemony advance.

quests.title: Power Core
quests.description: The Aethel Siphon Core is the planet's power source. The Hegemony will control the power. Enter and secure. Quest giver: Commander Drax at Bastion Core Command Bunker.

objectives.description: Travel to AETHEL SIPHON CORE. (Travel to: Aethel Siphon Core, Shattered Heart)

quests.title: Core Purge
quests.description: Hostile entities infest the Core. (Location: Aethel Siphon Core, Commander Drax) Purge them. The Core must be secure for Hegemony power operations.

objectives.desc: Purge hostile entities from the Core.

quests.title: Behemoth Defense
quests.description: Corrupted Behemoths assault the Core conduits. (Location: Aethel Siphon Core, Commander Drax) Defend the Core. Hegemony never surrenders a position.

quests.title: Rift Patrol
quests.description: Rift Stalkers use micro-rifts for ambush tactics. (Location: Aethel Siphon Core, Commander Drax) Counter-ambush them. Hegemony soldiers do not get ambushed.

objectives.description: Collect rift claw fragment (x1). [Item: Rift Claw Fragment]

quests.title: Titan Mobilization
quests.description: Intelligence confirms: the Gravity Titan is mobilizing. (Location: Aethel Siphon Core, Commander Drax) The Hegemony prepares for the largest military operation in our history.

objectives.desc: Defeat a Jotunn scout for the Titan.

quests.title: Toppling the Titan
quests.description: MAXIMUM PRIORITY: OPERATION TITANFALL. (Location: Cradle Expanse, Commander Drax) The Gravity Titan threatens to collapse Xylos. The Iron Hegemony will topple it. This is the largest military engagement in Hegemony history. Every soldier. Every tank. Every ounce of steel. BRING IT DOWN.

objectives.desc: Secure the Titan Core for Hegemony weapons research.
objectives.description: Collect raw aethel fragment (x1). [Item: Raw Aethel Fragment]

quests.title: The Hegemony Forge
quests.description: The Prima Materia Forge will become the Hegemony's primary weapons factory. Secure it for mass production. Quest giver: Foreman Brigg at Titan Assembly Yard Command Post.

objectives.description: Travel to PRIMA MATERIA FORGE. (Travel to: Prima Materia Forge, Shattered Heart)

quests.title: Defense Neutralization
quests.description: Ancient Forge defenses still operate. (Location: Prima Materia Forge, Commander Drax) Neutralize them. The Forge serves the Hegemony now.

quests.title: Forge Purification
quests.description: Corrupted Behemoths contaminate the Forge. (Location: Prima Materia Forge, Foreman Brigg) Purify the facility. Hegemony weapons require pure materials.

quests.title: Abyssal Purge
quests.description: Abyssal forces attempt to claim the Forge. (Location: Prima Materia Forge, Commander Drax) Purge them with maximum force.

quests.title: Jotunn Foundry
quests.description: Jotunns use the Forge's heat as weapons. (Location: Prima Materia Forge, Foreman Brigg) Disarm and destroy them. The Forge's fire belongs to the Hegemony.

quests.title: Apex Production
quests.description: Operation Apex Production begins. (Location: Prima Materia Forge, Foreman Brigg) The Forge will mass-produce Hegemony Apex-class weapons. Secure the production floor.

objectives.description: Collect raw aethel fragment (x1). [Item: Raw Aethel Fragment]

quests.title: Automaton Override
quests.description: Titan Forge Automatons resist our control. (Location: Prima Materia Forge, Foreman Brigg) Override them — by any means necessary.

quests.title: Proto Core Recycling
quests.description: Titan Proto Cores are failed Titans — but their materials are valuable. (Location: Prima Materia Forge, Foreman Brigg) Recycle them into Hegemony steel.

quests.title: Supreme General Trial
quests.description: The final trial before Supreme General rank. (Location: Prima Materia Forge, Commander Drax) Defeat both Titan and Harbinger in a single campaign. Show the galaxy what Hegemony steel can do.

quests.title: Supreme General
quests.description: High Commander Varkas himself will pin the Supreme General insignia on your chest. (Location: Bastion Core, Commander Drax) Return to the Bastion. The Hegemony salutes you.

objectives.description: Travel to BASTION CORE. (Travel to: Bastion Core)

quests.title: Apex Predator
quests.description: The Hidden Aethelgardian City contains Precursor weapons technology. The Hegemony will not allow any faction to claim it first. Quest giver: Commander Drax at Bastion Core Command Bunker.

objectives.description: Travel to HIDDEN AETHELGARDIAN CITY. (Travel to: Hidden Aethelgardian City)

quests.title: City Garrison
quests.description: The City has automated defenses. (Location: Hidden Aethelgardian City, Commander Drax) Destroy them. Hegemony takes what it wants.

objectives.description: Collect rift claw fragment (x3). [Item: Rift Claw Fragment]

quests.title: Weapons Cache
quests.description: The City holds Precursor weapons caches. (Location: Hidden Aethelgardian City, Commander Drax) Secure them all for the Hegemony arsenal.

objectives.description: Collect void channeler core (x5). [Item: Void Channeler Core]

objectives.desc: Eliminate forces drawn to the weapons.

quests.title: Apex Drake Elimination
quests.description: The Ashen Drake has evolved to Apex form. (Location: Shattered Heart, Commander Drax) Eliminate it. Hegemony tolerates no rivals — especially winged ones.

quests.title: Apex Harbinger Destruction
quests.description: The Abyss Harbinger has been reborn. (Location: Void Echo Chasm, Commander Drax) Destroy it again. And again if necessary. Hegemony victory is permanent.

quests.title: Apex Titan Fall
quests.description: The Gravity Titan manifests once more. (Location: Cradle Expanse, Commander Drax) Hegemony response unchanged: destroy it. Again.

quests.title: Tri-Threat Offensive
quests.description: All three Apex entities — Drake, Titan, Harbinger. (Location: Prima Materia Forge, Commander Drax) The Hegemony will destroy them all in a single campaign. Triple offensive authorized.

quests.title: Core Command
quests.description: The Aethel Siphon Core becomes Hegemony Central Command. Establish full military control of the planetary power grid. Quest giver: Commander Drax at Bastion Core Command Bunker.

objectives.description: Travel to AETHEL SIPHON CORE. (Travel to: Aethel Siphon Core, Shattered Heart)

quests.title: World Engine Subjugation
quests.description: The World Engine can reshape Xylos. (Location: Aethel Siphon Core, Commander Drax) The Hegemony will make it our ultimate weapon. Secure it absolutely.

quests.title: Engine Activation
quests.description: Activate the World Engine. (Location: Aethel Siphon Core, Commander Drax) This is the moment Hegemony dominance becomes absolute. Defend the activation at all costs.

quests.title: Final Purge
quests.description: With the Engine online, remaining hostiles are flushed to the surface. (Location: Severance Faultline, Commander Drax) Execute the final purge at the Faultline.

quests.title: Void Annihilation
quests.description: The Void is collapsing. (Location: Void Echo Chasm, Commander Drax) Annihilate the remaining void forces. The Hegemony will erase the Abyss from history.

quests.title: Tyrant Extermination
quests.description: Crystal Tyrants make their last stand. (Location: Shattered Heart, Commander Drax) Exterminate them. The Hegemony leaves no enemy standing.

quests.title: Forge Final Production
quests.description: The Forge enters final mass production. (Location: Prima Materia Forge, Foreman Brigg) Eliminate the last remaining threats to production.

quests.title: Expanse Conquest
quests.description: The Cradle Expanse is now Hegemony sovereign territory. Enforce it. Quest giver: Commander Drax at Bastion Core Command Bunker.

quests.title: Orbital Command
quests.description: From orbit, the Hegemony watches over our conquered world. (Location: Orbital Sentinel Command, Commander Drax) Maintain orbital supremacy forever.

quests.title: The Final Campaign
quests.description: The Hegemony launches the Final Campaign to eliminate all significant threats. (Location: Prima Materia Forge, Commander Drax) Channel your command authority into the war effort.

quests.title: The Final Conquest
quests.description: Only the ultimate conquest remains. (Location: Prima Materia Forge, Commander Drax) Face the three Apex entities — Jotunn, Drake, Harbinger. The Hegemony conquers all.

quests.title: Eternal Hegemon Trial
quests.description: The deepest Void Echo Chasm holds the final test. Defeat the eternal forms of Titan and Harbinger in single combat. The Hegemony demands nothing less than total victory. Quest giver: Commander Drax at Bastion Core Command Bunker.

objectives.description: Travel to VOID ECHO CHASM. (Travel to: Void Echo Chasm)

quests.title: Eternal Hegemon
quests.description: You have conquered everything. (Location: Bastion Core, Commander Drax) Titan. Harbinger. Drake. All have fallen to Hegemony steel. Return to the Bastion as the ETERNAL HEGEMON. Your name will be forged into the Bastion's walls forever. Strength through Unity. Power through Conquest. Forever.

objectives.desc: Return to the Bastion as the Eternal Hegemon.
objectives.description: Travel to BASTION CORE. (Travel to: Bastion Core)

objectives.desc: Receive the Eternal Hegemon honors from High Commander Varkas.
objectives.description: Speak with npc hegemony commander. (Commander Drax at Bastion Core Command Bunker)
